2025 Pricing Guide

Fractional CMO Cost:
What You Should Expect to Pay in 2025

Real pricing ranges, what drives cost up or down, how to evaluate ROI, and Treetop's transparent starting-from rates. No games, no fake anchor pricing.

The Pricing Landscape

Fractional CMO rates by engagement scope.

The range is wide because scope varies enormously. Here's how to read the market.

Tier Monthly Range Hours / Week Best For
Strategic Advisor $3,000–$5,000 4–8 hrs Early-stage companies that need direction, not execution management. Limited strategic oversight only.
Fractional CMO $6,000–$12,000 10–16 hrs B2B companies $5M–$30M with a small team to manage. Full strategy plus team/vendor oversight.
Senior Fractional CMO $12,000–$18,000 16–24 hrs Larger teams, complex go-to-market, multi-product or multi-market companies.
Heavy Engagement $18,000–$25,000+ 20–32 hrs Near-full-time, typically companies $30M+ in active growth or transformation phases.

Rates reflect 2025 U.S. market. Remote engagements run 10–20% lower than in-market. Rates for specialized verticals (fintech, healthcare, industrial) trend higher.

Pricing Drivers

What moves the number
up or down.

Pushes Price Higher
Deep industry specialization (fintech, healthcare, enterprise SaaS)
Management of a large internal team or multiple agencies
Complex multi-channel demand gen (events, paid, ABM, content all running)
Board-level reporting and investor communications
Short-term urgency or active fundraise timeline
In-person presence required in a specific market
Keeps Price Lower
Fewer hours required (strategy only, execution handled internally)
Clear scope with well-defined deliverables
Longer initial commitment (6+ month contracts)
Remote-only engagement
Small team, straightforward GTM motion
Early-stage company with equity component
Equity vs. Cash

Should your fractional CMO
take equity?

Short answer: probably not if you're doing $5M+. Here's the full picture.

→
Equity makes sense at early stage
Pre-revenue or seed-stage companies often attract fractional talent with equity + reduced cash. If you're capital-constrained and the CMO genuinely believes in the business, a 0.2–0.5% grant vesting over 2–4 years is reasonable.
→
Cash is cleaner once you have revenue
At $5M+ ARR, most good fractional CMOs want cash. Equity arrangements complicate the relationship — misaligned on exit strategy, cap table concerns, vesting cliffs creating weird incentives at the wrong times.
→
Beware equity-heavy proposals
A fractional CMO pushing hard for significant equity at your stage is either (a) not confident they can deliver measurable results or (b) not actually fractional — they want founder-level comp without founder-level commitment.
ROI Framework

How to evaluate if the cost
makes financial sense.

Run the math before you sign. This is the framework we use with every prospective client.

Sample ROI Calculation · $10M ARR B2B Company
Current monthly pipeline (marketing-sourced)
$500K
Fractional CMO cost (monthly)
−$9,000
Conservative pipeline improvement at month 4 (15%)
+$75K/mo added pipeline
At 25% close rate, added revenue per month
+$18,750/mo revenue
ROI at conservative assumptions
2.1x in month 4, 5x+ at month 12

Pipeline improvement of 15% at month 4 is conservative. Companies that haven't had marketing leadership before often see 30–50% pipeline growth. The real question: what's the cost of not having this?

Treetop's Approach

Our pricing, stated plainly.

We don't do pricing games. No fake anchor prices, no mysterious "it depends" without a starting point.

Strategy Engagement
$6K/mo
10 hours/week. Strategy, planning, and team oversight. Best for companies with execution resources in place.
Full Fractional CMO ← Most Popular
$10K/mo
15 hours/week. Full leadership including team/vendor management, board reporting, and AI-native workflow implementation.
Heavy Engagement
$16K/mo
20+ hours/week. Near-full-time presence. Complex organizations, active fundraise, or transformation initiatives.

All engagements start with a 3-month pilot. Cancel with 30 days notice. No long-term contracts required.

FAQ

Common questions about
fractional CMO pricing.

How much does a fractional CMO cost per month?
Typically $3,000–$20,000 per month depending on scope, hours, and seniority. Most B2B companies in the $5M–$50M range land between $6,000 and $12,000/month for a meaningful engagement with real accountability.
What hourly rate does a fractional CMO charge?
Rates range from $150–$400/hour depending on experience and specialization. Most don't work pure-hourly — they package into monthly retainers for predictability. If you're being quoted hourly, ask for a monthly package instead.
Do fractional CMOs take equity?
Some do, most don't — especially at $5M+ revenue. Equity makes sense at pre-seed/seed stages where cash is tight. For companies with revenue, cash arrangements are cleaner. If offered, typical grants are 0.1–0.5% vesting over 2–4 years.
Is a fractional CMO worth the cost?
For most B2B companies at $5M–$50M, yes — if the alternative is no marketing leadership or an expensive full-time hire. Run the ROI math: even a 15% improvement in marketing-sourced pipeline usually pays back within 60 days at typical close rates and deal sizes.
How long do fractional CMO contracts last?
Most engagements start with a 3-month pilot, then move to rolling monthly. Average engagement length is 12–18 months. Companies graduate out when they hire full-time marketing leadership — which is the intended outcome for healthy, growing businesses.
